ops.body:
**Artifact-placement + recon deviations (session 2026-07-19):**

1. **conventions.md -> cc-notes doc, not tree markdown.** Per the artifact-placement directive (durable knowledge routes through cc-notes; the tree carries only published docs + source), the "daemon conventions" content lands as a cc-notes doc in the daemonkit store, not tree docs/conventions.md. README + per-track CHANGELOG edits stay in-tree. Per-track migration docs land as cc-notes docs in each consumer's store.

2. **"macOS 26 stay-active disclosure" has no implementation to document.** Recon found no macOS 26 / stay-active / disclosure API in daemonkit or captain-hook. The conventions doc records the gap explicitly plus the nearest real mechanisms (SMAppService approval flow, ProcessType=Background, LSUIElement, AssociatedBundleIdentifiers) rather than inventing a section.

3. **capt-hook docs -- authored + handed off, never shipped by this session.** desktop-helper.qmd + the session-reviewer.qmd cross-link are authored this session and delivered as a captain-hook-store handoff doc; the captain-hook (track-k) lane applies them post-ship after re-verifying against what actually shipped. Two applier decisions (NOT ours): (a) the dirty tree already carries a new "## [Unreleased]" block above [10.2.0] -- fold it into a dated section at ship (capt-hook uses dated sections, no Unreleased); (b) the "SKILL.md stamp" item is dropped -- no SKILL frontmatter carries a version/date stamp; the real doc delta already lives in the scanning-sessions reference files in the dirty tree (references/pr-workflow.md, references/review-cli.md).

4. **cc-orchestrate consumer CHANGELOG: no change.** Its migration was version-resolver-only and the observable behavior is already covered by the existing dev-eviction [Unreleased] bullet.
